title: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao

seo.title: Pineapple Bun Recipe: Authentic Bo Lo Bao at Home
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Chinese Pineapple Buns (Bo Lo Bao) with our easy step-by-step recipe. Enjoy the sweet, crumbly topping and soft bread at home!
seo.h1: Authentic Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Freshly baked authentic Chinese Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao) with its signature crumbly topping.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the irresistible aroma and taste of homemade Pineapple Buns, a classic Chinese bakery treat.

description: Pineapple Buns, or Bo Lo Bao, are a beloved Hong Kong bakery classic, known for their signature crispy, sweet, crumbly topping that perfectly resembles a pineapple's skin. Beneath this delightful crust lies a wonderfully soft and fluffy milk bread, creating a perfect contrast of textures. These iconic buns are a comforting treat, ideal for breakfast, a snack, or alongside a cup of milk tea.

instructions.title: Prepare the Bread Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine flour, sugar, yeast, and salt. Add lukewarm milk and beaten egg. Mix until a shaggy dough forms. Knead on a lightly floured surface for 5-7 minutes until smooth. Incorporate softened butter and knead for another 5-7 minutes until the dough is elastic and passes the windowpane test.

instructions.title: First Rise of the Dough
instructions.text: Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 60-90 minutes, or until doubled in size. This is a crucial step for soft buns.

instructions.title: Make the Pineapple Topping
instructions.text: While the dough rises, prepare the topping. In a bowl, combine flour, confectioners sugar, and milk powder. Cut in the chilled butter using your fingertips or a pastry blender until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Add the egg yolk and milk, mixing until a soft dough forms. Do not overmix. Wrap in plastic and chill in the refrigerator.

instructions.title: Shape the Buns and Apply Topping
instructions.text: Punch down the risen dough and divide it into 8 equal portions. Roll each portion into a smooth ball. Take a piece of chilled topping dough, flatten it into a disc (about 3-4 inches wide) between two sheets of plastic wrap. Carefully peel off one plastic sheet and place the topping disc over a dough ball. Gently press and smooth the topping over the top of the bun, leaving the bottom exposed. Use a knife to lightly score a crisscross pattern on the topping, mimicking a pineapple.

instructions.title: Second Rise and Preheat Oven
instructions.text: Arrange the shaped buns on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between them. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and let them rise in a warm place for another 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy. Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F) during the last 15 minutes of the second rise.

instructions.title: Bake the Pineapple Buns
instructions.text: Just before baking, gently brush the tops of the buns (only the topping, avoid the exposed dough) with the beaten egg wash. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and crispy, and the bread is cooked through. The scoring will become more prominent during baking.

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the baked buns from the oven and transfer them to a wire rack to cool slightly. Pineapple Buns are best enjoyed warm, allowing the contrast between the crispy topping and soft bread to truly shine. Serve immediately with a hot beverage.

tips: For the softest dough, use high-quality bread flour and ensure your milk is lukewarm, not hot, to activate the yeast properly without killing it.
When making the topping, work quickly with chilled butter to prevent it from melting too much, which helps create a crumbly texture. Do not overmix the topping dough.
Pineapple buns are best eaten fresh and warm. If storing, keep them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 days. Reheat gently in an oven or toaster oven to restore crispiness.
article:
The humble yet iconic Pineapple Bun, or Bo Lo Bao as it’s known in Cantonese, holds a special place in the hearts of many, from bustling Hong Kong bakeries to dim sum restaurants worldwide. This beloved Chinese pastry, with its distinctive crumbly, sweet topping and soft, fluffy interior, is a culinary masterpiece that evokes comfort and nostalgia. Despite its name, the traditional Pineapple Bun contains no actual pineapple; instead, its golden-brown, cracked topping resembles the fruit’s textured skin. If you’ve ever dreamed of recreating this bakery favorite in your own kitchen, you’re in the right place. This comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know to bake perfect Pineapple Buns from scratch, bringing the authentic taste of Chinese baking into your home.

## What is Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao?

Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao is a classic Chinese sweet bread, instantly recognizable by its unique appearance and delightful contrast of textures. The "Bo Lo" in its name refers to the pineapple-like pattern that forms on the crust during baking. This crust is made from a shortbread-like dough, rich in butter and sugar, which is spread over a soft, milky bread dough before baking. While many versions of the Pineapple Bun are plain inside, serving as a versatile canvas for butter or a cup of milk tea, modern variations often include a range of fillings. These can range from a simple slab of cold butter (creating the famous "Bo Lo Yau"), to sweet custard, red bean paste, or even savory char siu (barbecue pork). The magic lies in the interplay between the slightly crunchy, sweet topping and the tender, airy bread underneath, making each bite a delightful experience.

## Ingredients You Need

To create the perfect Pineapple Bun from scratch, you'll need a combination of pantry staples and a few specific items. Quality ingredients make a noticeable difference in the final taste and texture.

For the Bread Dough:
1.  300g bread flour
2.  40g granulated sugar
3.  3g instant dry yeast
4.  3g salt
5.  1 large egg
6.  120ml warm milk (around 100-110°F / 38-43°C)
7.  30g unsalted butter, softened

For the Pineapple Topping:
1.  100g all-purpose flour
2.  60g unsalted butter, softened
3.  60g granulated sugar
4.  1 large egg yolk
5.  1/4 teaspoon baking powder
6.  1/2 teaspoon milk (optional, for consistency)

For the Egg Wash:
1.  1 egg yolk, beaten with 1 teaspoon water

Optional Filling (e.g., Custard or Plain):
1.  If making plain buns, have extra softened butter ready for serving.
2.  If making custard, prepare your favorite recipe in advance and chill.

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Making Pineapple Buns involves several stages, but each step is straightforward and rewarding. Patience is key, especially during proofing.

1.  Prepare the Bread Dough: In a large bowl, combine bread flour, sugar, yeast, and salt. Add the egg and warm milk, mixing until a shaggy dough forms. Turn it out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for about 10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Incorporate the softened butter and continue kneading for another 5-7 minutes until the dough is smooth, pliable, and passes the windowpane test. Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1-1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.
2.  Make the Pineapple Topping: While the dough is proofing, prepare the topping. In a medium bowl, cream together the softened butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in the egg yolk. Sift in the all-purpose flour and baking powder, mixing until just combined to form a soft dough. If the dough is too stiff, add a tiny bit of milk. Wrap the topping dough in plastic wrap and chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.
3.  Divide and Shape the Bread Dough: Once the bread dough has doubled, gently punch it down to release the air. Divide the dough into 8-10 equal portions (about 60-70g each). Shape each portion into a smooth ball. Place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between each.
4.  Prepare and Apply the Topping: Take the chilled topping dough and divide it into the same number of portions as your bread dough balls. On a lightly floured surface or between two sheets of parchment paper, roll out each portion of topping dough into a flat circle, large enough to cover the top of a bread dough ball. Carefully place a topping circle over each bread dough ball, gently pressing down to adhere it, but not too firmly as to flatten the bun. Use a dough scraper or knife to score a crisscross pattern on the topping; this helps create the signature pineapple texture.
5.  Second Proofing: Cover the shaped buns loosely with plastic wrap and let them rise in a warm place for another 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy.
6.  Bake the Pineapple Buns: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Just before baking, gently brush the egg wash over the topping of each bun. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and the buns are cooked through. The beautiful crackled surface and golden hue are what make the Pineapple Bun so appealing.
7.  Cool and Serve: Transfer the baked buns to a wire rack to cool slightly. Serve warm, plain, or with your desired filling.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that signature crispy, sweet crust on your Pineapple Bun takes practice, but these tips will help you perfect your technique:

*   Kneading is Key: Don't skimp on kneading the bread dough. Proper gluten development is crucial for a soft, airy interior. The windowpane test (stretching a small piece of dough until it's thin enough to see light through without tearing) is a good indicator.
*   Temperature Control: For yeast activation and dough rising, ensure your milk is warm (not hot), and your proofing environment is consistently warm and draft-free.
*   Topping Consistency: The topping dough should be soft but not sticky. If it’s too warm, it will be difficult to roll; if too cold, it will crack. Adjust with a tiny bit of milk or flour as needed.
*   Gentle Handling: When applying the topping and scoring, be gentle. You don't want to deflate the proofed bread dough underneath.
*   Oven Temperature: An accurate oven temperature is vital for even baking and proper crust formation. Consider using an oven thermometer.
*   Even Scoring: Score the topping evenly and not too deeply. This helps in achieving the appealing cracked "pineapple" pattern without cutting into the bread dough.

## Serving Suggestions

Pineapple Buns are incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in many ways. The most classic pairing is with a thick slice of cold butter inserted into a freshly baked, still-warm bun – this is known as "Bo Lo Yau" and offers a delightful contrast of temperatures and textures. They are also perfect alongside a cup of Hong Kong-style milk tea or coffee for breakfast or afternoon tea. For a heartier snack, consider filling them with sweet custard, red bean paste, or even savory char siu pork. The plain Pineapple Bun is also wonderful on its own, allowing the subtle sweetness of the bread and the rich topping to shine.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the traditional Pineapple Bun is a classic, there are many ways to customize it to your taste or dietary needs:

*   Fillings Galore: Experiment with fillings! Beyond butter, custard, and red bean, you could try taro paste, coconut filling, or even a cream cheese filling for a modern twist. For a savory option, a small amount of pre-cooked char siu pork makes a fantastic surprise.
*   Gluten-Free Option: While challenging, you can experiment with gluten-free bread flour blends for the dough, though the texture will differ. The topping can often be adapted more easily with gluten-free flour.
*   Dairy-Free: Substitute milk with a plant-based milk (like almond or soy) and butter with a good quality dairy-free butter alternative in both the dough and topping.
*   Less Sweet: Reduce the sugar slightly in both the bread dough and the topping if you prefer a less sweet bun. However, remember the sugar contributes to the texture and browning.
*   Enriched Dough: For an even richer bread dough, you can add an extra egg yolk or a bit more butter.

Baking your own Pineapple Buns is a truly rewarding culinary journey. From the satisfying process of kneading the dough to the intoxicating aroma filling your kitchen, and finally, that first bite of a warm, homemade bun, it’s an experience that connects you to a rich tradition of Chinese baking. Don't be intimidated by the steps; with a little practice and these detailed instructions, you’ll be churning out bakery-quality Bo Lo Bao in no time. Enjoy the process and savor the delicious results of your efforts!
